mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware for deploying and orchestrating AI agents via A2A and MCP protocols on Kubernetes. It provides a standardized approach to agent communication, security, and management. Kagenti is installed via OCI-based Helm charts from GitHub Container Registry.

steps.title: Verify prerequisites
steps.description:
Ensure you have the required tools installed:

```bash
kubectl version --client
helm version
```

> Requires kubectl >= 1.32.1 and Helm >= 3.18.0 (Helm v4 is not supported). You also need a Kubernetes cluster with admin access.

steps.title: Install Kagenti dependencies
steps.description:
Install the Kagenti dependencies chart, which sets up required operators and CRDs:

```bash
helm install kagenti-deps oci://ghcr.io/kagenti/kagenti/kagenti-deps --namespace kagenti --create-namespace
```

> This installs dependencies like the Kagenti operator and webhook components.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
Install the main Kagenti chart from the OCI registry:

```bash
helm install kagenti oci://ghcr.io/kagenti/kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti
```

> For custom values, download the default values file from the repo: https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/tree/main/charts/kagenti

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check that all Kagenti components are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagenti
kubectl get svc -n kagenti
```

> You should see the Kagenti operator, webhook, and core service pods in Running state.

steps.title: Optional: Install the TUI (terminal UI)
steps.description:
Kagenti provides a Go-based terminal UI that must be built from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti.git
cd kagenti
make install-tui
```

> The TUI binary will be installed to your GOPATH/bin. There are no pre-built CLI binaries in GitHub releases.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
Remove the Kagenti deployment and dependencies:

```bash
helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti
helm uninstall kagenti-deps --namespace kagenti
kubectl delete namespace kagenti
```

> Uninstall the main chart first, then dependencies.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Upgrade to a newer version by specifying the OCI chart version:

```bash
kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml
helm upgrade kagenti-deps oci://ghcr.io/kagenti/kagenti/kagenti-deps --namespace kagenti
helm upgrade kagenti oci://ghcr.io/kagenti/kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti
kubectl get pods -n kagenti
```

> Always backup before upgrading. Upgrade dependencies first, then the main chart.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig and cluster connectivity with `kubectl cluster-info`.

troubleshooting.title: OCI chart pull fails
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Authentication may be required for GHCR.

**Fix:**
Log in to GHCR: `helm registry login ghcr.io -u <github-username> -p <github-token>`

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti` to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Dependencies chart fails to install
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti operator or webhook CRDs may not be available.

**Fix:**
Check that the OCI artifacts are accessible: `helm show chart oci://ghcr.io/kagenti/kagenti/kagenti-deps`

versionNotes.changes: Stable release with A2A and MCP protocol support for AI agent orchestration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: Install via OCI Helm charts: first `kagenti-deps` then `kagenti` from `oci://ghcr.io/kagenti/kagenti/`. Verify with `kubectl get pods -n kagenti`.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with admin access. Helm v4 is not supported. For local development, Kind with Docker Desktop (16GB RAM, 4 cores recommended).
